content.description:
Every item has an `attachments` array that establishes composition relationships. Attachments represent "other items that are relevant to this item". The nature of the relevance is not specified: the spatial view chooses to interpret attachments as child windows; other views will have their own interpretation.

## Attachments Array Structure

Attachments are objects with an `id` and a `view` configuration:

```javascript
attachments: [
  {
    id: "attachment-item-uuid",
    view: {
      type: "view-uuid",  // Required: which view renders this attachment
      // ... view-specific configuration
    },
    previousView: { ... }  // Optional: for view toggling
  }
]
```

## The `view` Object

The `view` object specifies how this parent displays this child:

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|--------|
| `type` | **Required.** UUID of the view to use |
| (others) | View-specific configuration |

### Spatial Container Views

For [spatial-canvas-view](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9), the view object includes positioning:

```javascript
view: {
  type: "spatial-canvas-view-uuid",
  x: 100,
  y: 50,
  width: 400,
  height: 300,
  z: 1,
  minimized: false,
  maximized: false,
  pinned: false
}
```

### List Views

For list views, the view object might just have `type`:

```javascript
view: { type: "compact-view-uuid" }
```

## Managing Attachments

The API provides attachment management functions:

```javascript
// Add an attachment
await api.attach(parentId, attachmentId, viewConfig?);

// Remove an attachmen
await api.detach(parentId, attachmentId);

// Update child's view configuration
await api.updateViewConfig(parentId, attachmentId, { x: 200, y: 100 });

// Change which view renders an attachment
await api.setAttachmentView(parentId, attachmentId, newViewId);
```

## View Toggling with `previousView`

When switching an attachment's view, the old configuration is preserved:

```javascript
// Before toggle
{
  id: "item-1",
  view: { type: "expanded-view", ... },
  previousView: null
}

// After toggle to compact view
{
  id: "item-1",
  view: { type: "compact-view" },
  previousView: { type: "expanded-view", ... }  // Preserved!
}

// Toggle back restores expanded-view with its configuration
```

## Contextual vs Preferred Views

Attachments can be displayed differently in different parents:

- **Contextual view** (in parent's `view.type`): "How this parent shows this attachment"
- **Preferred view** (on item itself): "How this item prefers to be shown"

Contextual overrides preferred. See [kernel:view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) for the full resolution hierarchy.

## Ordering

The `attachments` array is **ordered**. Array position determines:
- Display order in lists
- Z-index base in spatial layouts (higher index = rendered later = on top by default)

## Cycle Prevention

`api.attach()` checks for cycles:
- A cannot be an attachment of itself
- A cannot be an attachment of B if B is already a descendant of A

This prevents infinite rendering loops.

## Cascade Deletion

When an item is deleted:
1. It's removed from all parents' `attachments` arrays
2. The item itself is deleted from storage
3. Its own attachments are **not** deleted (they become orphans)

Orphan items still exist but aren't reachable through navigation. Use search to find them.

## Design Rationale

### Why No Special Container Type?

There is no special "container" concept in the kernel. A container is simply an item whose view happens to render attachments. This means:
- Any item can contain other items
- Container behavior is a view concern, not a data model concern
- Hierarchical composition emerges from simple rules

### Why Graph Structure (Not Tree)?

Items can appear in multiple containers simultaneously. A note can be in both "Project X" and "Meeting Notes." This requires a DAG (directed acyclic graph) rather than a strict tree. Cycle prevention ensures rendering terminates.

### Why Position Data in Attachments?

Position data could live in a separate layout table, but embedding it in the attachment reference avoids synchronization issues (child removed but layout entry remains). Single source of truth.

## See Also

- [kernel:item](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — The item structure including attachments
- [Spatial Windowing](item://c0c0c0c0-0020-0000-0000-000000000000) — How attachments are rendered spatially
- [Cycle-Safe Traversal](item://c0c0c0c0-0013-0000-0000-000000000000) — Handling cycles in the attachment graph
- [Transclusion](item://c0c0c0c0-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) — Inline embedding (different from attachments)
- [REPL](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — Testing attachment operations via the API
